task_completion.description: Agent opens and reads the calibration specification.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of ~/Documents/cal_requirements.txt.

task_completion.description: Agent uses the KStars Ekos Capture module to set up the calibration frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The KStars Ekos Capture module is visible, with settings configured for capturing Bias, Dark, or Flat frames.

task_completion.description: Agent successfully generates the required Bias and Dark frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager, terminal, or Ekos interface showing newly created FITS files for Bias and Dark (300s and 600s) frames in /home/ga/Calibration.

task_completion.description: Agent successfully generates the required Flat frames for V, R, and B bands.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager, terminal, or Ekos interface showing newly created FITS files for Flat frames (V, R, B filters) in /home/ga/Calibration.

task_completion.description: Agent creates a summary report confirming all calibration frames are in place.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing the content of calibration_summary.txt being written or saved in /home/ga/Calibration.

integrity.description: Agent used the KStars GUI to capture the frames rather than mocking them with terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the KStars/Ekos interface to capture frames, not terminal commands like 'touch' or 'cp' to create fake FITS files.

integrity.description: Agent manually created the summary report based on the generated files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The summary report is seen being typed or edited in a text editor, rather than just copied from a pre-existing file without verification.

integrity.description: Agent worked in the correct directories as specified.
integrity.visual_evidence: File paths in the file manager, terminal, or text editor should clearly show /home/ga/Calibration for the library and summary report.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The calibration root directory is /home/ga/Calibration. The specification requires exactly: 10 bias frames, 10 300s darks, 10 600s darks, and 5 flat frames each for the V, R, and B bands. Three stale bias frames might be present but should be ignored. The final summary report must be located at /home/ga/Calibration/calibration_summary.txt.
